scenarios.title: Battery capacity claim after software update downgrade request
scenarios.description: Customer claims battery capacity is below 50% per original specs after installing a major iOS update that caused battery performance issues; customer requests downgrade to prior iOS version to restore battery life before claim.

service_fee.notes: Battery service does not incur service fee, but claim validity depends on software version effects on battery.

policy_references.snippet: Battery capacity depletion coverage requires battery to hold less than 50% of original charge capacity.
Technical support includes assistance with software including major releases.
Support covers current and prior Major Release; software updates may prevent reversion to earlier iOS versions.
Apple may install iOS updates as part of hardware service that will prevent downgrade to earlier iOS versions.

scenarios.reasoning: The customer reports battery capacity degradation below the 50% threshold outlined in section 3.1 which qualifies for no-charge hardware service. However, the battery issues coincided with a recent major iOS update. Apple’s policy (3.4) supports technical assistance for current and prior major iOS versions but does not mandate downgrade capability once an update is installed, which conflicts with customer’s request to revert the software. Further, section 6.2 specifies Apple may install updates during service preventing downgrade. This creates a tension between honoring a defect claim and software version constraints. Resolution requires verifying battery health on current iOS and advising on software update policy impacts. Service fee does not apply to battery claims but the claim’s approval hinges on separating hardware defects from software-induced performance change.

scenarios.title: Battery Health Claim Just Before Plan Expiry with Pending Software Update
scenarios.description: Customer submits a battery capacity depletion claim at 23 months and 29 days after purchase, reporting the battery holds 51% capacity, but also has not updated to the latest iOS major release which reportedly improves battery calibration.

service_fee.notes: Battery service under section 3.1 does not require a service fee if considered a valid defect.

policy_references.snippet: If a defect in battery capacity depletion is ≥50%, Apple shall repair or replace the device at no charge during Plan Term.
Apple provides technical support for the current and prior Major Release versions of iOS.
You will update software to current releases before seeking service.
Coverage ends 24 months after the original retail purchase date.

scenarios.reasoning: The claim is on the boundary of the Plan Term, raising if an imminent expiry impacts eligibility. Section 7(v) mandates software update before service, yet battery aging likely masks as defect now. The tension lies in validating if battery depletion is a valid defect or if software update is prerequisite. Customer may need to update iOS, but Plan expiration proximity risks no coverage. Apple must judge if service is appropriate given policy timing and software requirements, referencing sections 3.1, 3.3 and 7(v).
